title: Before you lead anyone
caption:
You want to lead. That's not the problem.

The problem is most men want the authority before they've done the work that earns it. Scripture doesn't hand out titles. It builds men.

4 traits God develops in a man before He trusts him with anyone else:

1. Serve where no one's watching. The man God promotes is the one nobody had to ask. He took out the trash, followed through on the small thing, didn't wait for a title to start doing the work. Faithfulness in small things is the only résumé God reads.

2. Tell the hard truth face to face. Most men talk around conflict — group chats, venting to everyone but the guy. "Better is open rebuke than hidden love." — Proverbs 27:5. The man who won't confront can't lead. He can only manage.

3. Go first into the cost. Jesus didn't ask his disciples to die for him. He died first. First to apologize. First to forgive. First to take the hit. The man worth following already paid.

4. Get under authority. The centurion understood power because he was under power — and Jesus marveled at that (Matthew 8). Who you submit to right now is forming who you'll lead tomorrow. You can't lead well what you haven't learned to follow.

—

Comment "lead" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS — the daily anchor for the man being built.

#ethos #christianmen #faithoverfear #godlymen #manofgod #leadership #disciplineequalsfreedom #masculinity #scripture #christianity
theme: He leads by going first into the cost.
